title: LICENSE
text:
The ZALF Living Labs Infrastructure Community makes all of our materials publicly available under open source licenses.
Documents and data are made available under a CC-BY license. Software are made available under an MIT license.
The license text listed below (describing both CC-BY and MIT licenses as well as their usage in the ZALF Living Labs Infrastructure community) is re-used under a CC-BY license from The Carpentries community materials. (Specifically from the Reproducible Science Curriculum).
You can read more about licenses in the book itself.


All documentation and materials in this repository are made available under the Creative Commons Attribution license. The following is a human-readable summary of (and not a substitute for) the full legal text of the CC BY 4.0 license.
You are free:

to Share—copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format
to Adapt—remix, transform, and build upon the material

for any purpose, even commercially.
The licensor cannot revoke these freedoms as long as you follow the license terms.
Under the following terms:

Attribution—You must give appropriate credit (mentioning that your work is derived from work that is Copyright © ZALF Living Labs Infrastructure community and, where practical, linking to https://github.com/zalf-lli), provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. You may do so in any reasonable manner, but not in any way that suggests the licensor endorses you or your use.
No additional restrictions—You may not apply legal terms or technological measures that legally restrict others from doing anything the license permits.

With the understanding that:

You do not have to comply with the license for elements of the material in the public domain or where your use is permitted by an applicable exception or limitation.
No warranties are given. The license may not give you all of the permissions necessary for your intended use. For example, other rights such as publicity, privacy, or moral rights may limit how you use the material.




Except where otherwise noted, the software and infrastructure provided by the The ZALF Living Labs Infrastructure Community are made available under the OSI-approved MIT license.

The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software.
THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED “AS IS”, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.

title: Resources and tools
text:
This repo is intended to be a living document for SWG and IAT members to share useful information and resources related to Open Science, programming and Data Visualisation.
Some documents that provide guiding principles:
Check out the Open Science Awesome List

title: Resources and tools
text:
Data visualisation

Fundamentals of Data Visualization
Open Visualisation Academy

title: Resources and tools
text:
R
What they forgot to teach you about R: https://rstats.wtf/

title: Resources and tools
text:
Quarto

Beautiful slides with RevealJS and Quarto

title: Resources and tools
text:
Tools and Software

EU alternatives for software - A directory of software alternatives to popular proprietary software, with a focus on European users and organizations.
gettingstuffdoneai - Directory of Generative AI Tools and Models, categorized by use case and functionality.
Inkscape - Open source vector graphics editor, alternative to Adobe products, useful for creating and editing figures for publications and presentations. Available via the ZALF baramundi client.

title: Resources and tools
text:
Training

Digital Research Academy - a trainer network focused on Open Science, Data Literacy, and Research Software Engineering (DRA’s Zenodo collection).
BERD academy
BERD reproducible research course
NFDI4Earth - Educational Portal
Assured Training -Ensures the safe, legal, and ethical use of sensitive data through targeted training and certification.
KI-campus - Numerous free courses on AI and related topics, including data science, machine learning, and ethical considerations in AI.
